codeReview.summary: Pass. I reviewed the original failed UX fix (`22e71f7`), the cache-invalidation fix commit (`49639ac`), and the current frontend read path. The original feature already bumped `refreshKey` on confirmed writes; this fix adds `forceRefresh` to `loadMarketSnapshot()` and makes `App.tsx` call it with `forceRefresh: refreshKey > 0`, so the post-write reload now bypasses the 10-second snapshot cache. Because `RecentActivitySection` renders `snapshot.recentActivity` from the same refreshed snapshot, the stale-read blocker called out in the prior review is addressed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill still has not incorporated the round-2 scrutiny guidance for refresh-related fixes. It tells workers to read `src/lib/readModel/` broadly and manually verify that forms render/accept input, but it still does not explicitly require inspecting cache-bearing read-model modules when a feature changes frontend refresh behavior.

summary: Pass. The fix closes the prior stale-read failure by bypassing the 10-second `loadMarketSnapshot()` cache on post-write refreshes, so confirmed writes now trigger a fresh snapshot/recent-activity read instead of serving the cached payload. Shared-state follow-up: the frontend-worker skill still needs the earlier scrutiny guidance about explicitly checking cache-owning read-model modules for refresh fixes.
